meaning: it (referring to what was mentioned before)
do you then dispute with him?
The Lat (as the object being discussed)
and fate (the name of an idol)
if (introducing the condition that they follow only assumptions)
it (referring to the false idols/names mentioned after)
they follow closely/earnestly (referring to the disbelievers following false desires)
and indeed (connects to previous statement about false assumptions)
the assumption (that they follow instead of knowledge)
so that he may give what is due
and he gives what is due
and the many very bad/shameful things
except (what comes after in the ayah)
the earth (being described in this ayah)
and that (connects this thought to the previous one)
the ultimate end (referring to the final destination with Allah)
He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
and he caused to live/gave life
from a single drop of fluid (emphasized)
and indeed/and that (connects to previous statement)
and the overturned one (the city that was destroyed)
He made (it) fall (referring to Allah destroying cities)
and then he covered it completely
The Approaching One (referring to the Day of Judgment)
